channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: USAID security leaders on leave after trying to keep Musk's DOGE from classified info, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has placed two top security chiefs at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave after they refused to turn over classified material in restricted areas to Elon Musk's government-inspection teams, a current and a former U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US-backed commander says his Kurdish-led group wants a secular and civil state in post-Assad Syria
item.description: HASSAKEH, Syria (AP) — The commander of the main U.S.-backed force in Syria said Sunday the recent ouster of the Assad family from power should be followed by building a secular, civil and decentralized state that treats all its citizens equally no matter their religion or ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Gaza war is paused, but Israel and Hamas keep up a fierce battle of optics over hostage releases
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Arbel Yehoud is achingly close to freedom, but her face is a study of shadow and terror. She is dwarfed by dozens of masked Hamas fighters — and beyond them, a heaving mob of chanting men who surround her and fill the frame.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI employees questioned about involvement in Jan. 6 cases as Justice Department weighs firings
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of FBI employees who participated in investigations related to the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter wins posthumous Grammy for narrating an audiobook of his Sunday school lessons
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former President Jimmy Carter has won a posthumous Grammy award. Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, died in December at age 100.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Fans boo US national anthem at NHL game in Canada amid trade war
item.description: The booing came shortly after Trump signed an order imposing stiff tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flight headed to LaGuardia evacuated after engine issue: FAA
item.description: Passengers were taken back to the terminal after deplaning via slides and stairs, according to a spokesperson for United Airlines.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows wing of United Airlines plane catching fire during takeoff at Houston airport
item.description: Wild video captures the moment a United Airlines flight from Houston to New York was delayed after a wing on the plane caught fire during takeoff on Sunday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Passengers, crew killed in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia identified
item.description: Authorities said a seventh person, who had been in a car at the time of the crash, has died. Their identity has not yet been released.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina retires jersey number of three-time WNBA MVP A’ja Wilson
item.description: Three-time WNBA MVP A’ja Wilson's South Carolina No. 22 jersey was hung in the rafters Sunday at Colonial Life Arena, her college home court that already features a statue of the program’s all-time leading scorer outside the main entrance.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Families visit DC crash site days after the deadliest US air disaster in a generation
item.description: Dozens of people walked along the banks of the Potomac River near Reagan National Airport, close to where an American Airlines jet and an Army Black Hawk helicopter collided Wednesday, killing all 67 aboard.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to watch and stream the Grammy Awards ceremony, red carpet arrivals and interviews
item.description: Here is what you need to know about the awards show, including how to watch or stream live and see the stars arrive on the red carpet.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Airport employee injured in collision with aircraft wing at Chicago's O'Hare
item.description: The driver of a tug vehicle at Chicago O'Hare International Airport was injured Saturday evening after colliding with the wing of an airplane.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Ohtani's return to pitching could come in May for World Series champion Dodgers
item.description:
Shohei Ohtani pitching for the Los Angeles Dodgers in May is "about right,” manager Dave Roberts said Saturday at the team's annual fan fest.
He reiterated that Ohtani won't pitch in the season-opening Japan Series against the Chicago Cubs on March 18-19 in Tokyo.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Officials say remains of 55 of 67 victims of midair collision have been recovered and identified
item.description: Authorities say they have recovered the remains of 55 of the 67 people killed in the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001.
item.dc:creator: David R. Martin And Claudia Lauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ICE nabs another suspected Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang member in Aurora, Colorado
item.description: ICE Denver and Homeland Security Investigations officers arrested another suspected Tren de Aragua (TdA) gang member in Aurora, Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein files: Trump's election could expose names on 'black book' list
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein's 'black book' and client list could be closer to public release thanks to new leadership in Washington, D.C., under President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OJ Simpson murder trial: Suppressed witness testimony casts shadow over verdict
item.description: Witnesses say they saw OJ Simpson driving near the crime scene without his headlights on and dumping a wrapped item in a trash can the night of the crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luxury Florida mall's string of murders and kidnapping have one thing in common: retired FBI agent
item.description: It’s been 18 years since Boca Raton, Florida, was rocked by two murder cases and a kidnapping at a local mall, and a retired FBI agent believes the unsolved crimes are connected.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army releases name of 3rd soldier who died in helicopter and airliner crash
item.description: The Army on Saturday released the name of the third soldier who died Wednesday when an Army helicopter collided with an American Airlines jet near Reagan National Airport in D.C.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to settle with woman who says ex-Officer Chauvin knelt on her neck
item.description: The city of Minneapolis has agreed to pay $600,000 to settle a lawsuit by a woman who alleged that ex-Officer Derek Chauvin hauled her from her minivan and pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just as he did four months later when he killed George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Data from the deadliest U.S. air accident in a generation show conflicting altitude readings
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Costco, Teamsters reach tentative contract agreement, avoiding a strike
item.description: Costco and the Teamsters union have reached a tentative agreement on a new contract, avoiding a strike, the union said Saturday. Teamsters spokesman Matthew McQuaid confirmed the agreement, which will have to be approved by members. Details of the agreement weren't immediately available.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he ordered precision military strike against senior ISIS planner
item.description: President Trump said he ordered precision air strikes on Saturday morning targeting a "senior ISIS Attack planner" and others the unnamed individual recruited and led in Somalia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Colleges around the US cautiously navigate Trump's DEI crackdown
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — In Boston, Northeastern University renamed a program for underrepresented students, emphasizing “belonging” for all. In New Jersey, a session at Rutgers University catering to students from historically Black colleges had to be abruptly canceled. And around the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An air ambulance has crashed in Philadelphia. What to know about air medical transport
item.description: A medical transport jet carrying a child patient, her mother and four others slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood and exploded in flames.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Black box recovered in Philadelphia medical transport jet crash: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder was located at the site of initial impact, at a depth of 8 feet, according to the NTSB.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: Cartels authorized the use of weaponized drones against ICE agents
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection is taking threats seriously enough to circulate memos among agents.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of Costco workers prepare to strike with midnight contract deadline looming
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers prepared to strike with a midnight deadline drawing closer Friday while the worker's union and the company negotiated. The Teamsters union represents 18,000 Costco workers in six states: California, Washington, Maryland, Virginia, New Jersey and New York.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Department of Education undoes Biden's Title IX rules on gender
item.description: The Department of Education announced Friday it is scrapping former President Biden's 2024 Title IX changes, getting rid of discrimination protection based on gender identity and sexual orientation and changing how sexual assault cases are handled on campus.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Numerous goods could get more expensive due to Trump tariffs, experts say
item.description: The 25% tax that President Donald Trump plans to slap on imports from Canada and Mexico as soon as Saturday could drive up the price of everything from gasoline and pickup trucks, to Super Bowl party guacamole dip.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN AP Economics Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Carolina death row inmate maintains his innocence and won't ask for clemency
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — After the U.S. Supreme Court rejected the final appeal of a South Carolina inmate, he decided not to ask the governor for clemency, saying he didn't want to spend the rest of his life in prison for a murder he has always insisted he did not commit. Marion Bowman Jr.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4
